config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak sewenang-wenang. Dari pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, padahal bisa merugikan orang kecil atau kelompok minoritas. Bagi saya, yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat selalu di atas individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily become a blank check for power. From my perspective as someone from China, I have seen how strongly people value social stability and collective goals, and I understand why many people put them first, especially in times of crisis or development. But I also think individual rights need real protection, because without clear limits, “the common good” can be used to silence ordinary people or ignore minority interests. The better principle is balance: society matters, but it should not be allowed to crush the basic rights of the person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” memang sering dipakai, tetapi tidak jarang jadi pembenaran untuk menekan orang kecil, warga adat, atau minoritas yang suaranya lemah. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari hak individu yang dilindungi, karena kalau hak dasar mudah dikorbankan, kekuasaan jadi terlalu gampang menyalahgunakan wewenang. Jadi, yang lebih tepat adalah keseimbangan yang ketat: kepentingan bersama penting, tetapi tidak boleh menghapus martabat dan hak dasar setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because that easily turns into a justification for suppressing ordinary people. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why collective interests and social stability are valued, especially in a country that has focused so much on development and order, but I have also seen how “for the greater good” can become a convenient excuse to ignore weaker voices. The point is not to deny society, but to set firm limits: public interests matter, yet basic rights should not be sacrificed whenever they become inconvenient. If a society cannot protect individuals from overreach, then its claim to serve the common good is not very convincing.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyerobotan tanah, pembungkaman kritik, atau menekan kelompok yang suaranya lemah, jadi saya tidak percaya pada klaim itu tanpa batas yang sangat ketat. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang berani melindungi hak dasar tiap orang, termasuk minoritas dan warga kecil. Jadi bagi saya, yang benar bukan mengalahkan individu, melainkan menyeimbangkan kepentingan publik dengan perlindungan hak yang tidak boleh ditawar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim as a general principle, because in practice it often becomes an excuse to weaken ordinary people’s protections. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why many people value collective order, development, and social stability, and I do not dismiss those goals at all, but I have also seen how easily “the interests of society” can be used to override weaker voices. A society is not truly serving the public good if it can sacrifice individual rights whenever convenient. The right balance is to protect the common interest while drawing firm lines that the state and majority cannot cross.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
